finding_results.notes: The chapter now routes every major fact through a named record source or visible on-page perceiver: access log, 911 audio, body-camera footage, scene photos, forensic worksheet, sign-in sheet, bridge-call recording, press PDF, notification script/call recording, analyst notes, and launch email/page capture. The free-floating later-filings and generalized report language were removed.

finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_18.md:9; chapters/chapter_18.md:11; chapters/chapter_18.md:23; chapters/chapter_18.md:27; chapters/chapter_18.md:33; chapters/chapter_18.md:47; chapters/chapter_18.md:95; chapters/chapter_18.md:101; chapters/chapter_18.md:109; chapters/chapter_18.md:125; chapters/chapter_18.md:137
finding_results.notes: Expanded the facilities discovery into real-time dock/cafeteria/service-corridor action and rendered the family-notification beat through a recorded call with quoted language, sensory residue, and immediate consequence. The launch aftermath remains on-page through the internal email and archived product-page capture rather than a summary gloss.

Lena's body now reflects the Chapter 17 fight choreography: the broken shelving is driven into the right side of her thorax and the worksheet labels it penetrating thoracic trauma. Noor's wound direction was also aligned to the continuity sheet.
Verified heading contract remains intact and chapter length is 2675 words, exceeding the 2000-word minimum.

Replaced narrator paraphrase with brief quoted excerpts in four distinct institutional registers. The press release (line 87) now speaks in PR-hedging voice: passive construction, conditional sympathy, safety posturing, forward-looking commitments. The HR notification script (line 91) now reads as procedural call-center protocol: imperative verbs, approved-language references, scripted fallback responses, reference IDs. The analyst note (line 131) now speaks in financial-valuation register: BUY rating, price target, litigation headwind, enterprise adoption, risk-factor filing. The deployment email (line 135) now reads as corporate project-management cadence: schedule-specific timestamps, time zones, provisioned access, EOD. Each register is identifiable by diction and rhythm without labels. Existing dialogue voices (Diane Okafor's 911 call, officer body-camera speech, the parking-lot engineer, the Mariam Hassani notification call) were left untouched as they already carried distinct spoken texture.

Threaded Diane's sensory and cognitive experience through separated paragraphs in the opening discovery run so the documentary coda no longer stays in pure external report for more than a few paragraphs. Also aligned Noor's terminal clock to 4:47 PM at chapters/chapter_18.md:57 to match the chapter-17 handoff and continuity sheet.

pass_summaries.summary: Reframed Chapter 18 as a bounded aftermath record, turned the body discovery and Noor's family notification into present-action scenes, corrected the Lena/Noor wound continuity, and expanded the chapter well past the pass minimum while preserving the required final landing.

Four institutional voices that were collapsed into neutral narrator paraphrase now speak in their own registers through brief quoted excerpts: PR statement hedges in corporate sympathy, HR script deflects in imperative procedure, analyst note financializes in valuation language, deployment email proceeds in bloodless schedule cadence. The revision is tightly scoped to the four artifact sections flagged by the finding; all existing dialogue, scene detail, and chapter architecture remain unchanged. All must-land beats preserved.

The opening documentary sequence now includes repeated embodied access through Diane's smell, self-assessment, and momentary cognitive refusal, preserving the formal coda while restoring fictionality to the report-heavy stretch.

summary: Merged three-pass revision report (FIXED=6, PARTIAL=0, UNRESOLVED=0).
